title: The first dish my host mother cooked in Tbilisi
summary: A short story about the first home-cooked meal you ate at a host family abroad.
topic: The first dish my host mother cooked in Tbilisi
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about the first dish a host family or local friend cooked for you abroad. Describe the kitchen, the food, and what it meant. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: On my first evening in Tbilisi, my host mother…
While she was rolling out the dough, she told me…
I did not understand most of her words, but…
sampleText: On my first evening in Tbilisi, my host mother, Nino, was already standing at the stove when I came in with my suitcase. She greeted me with a hand on each cheek and pointed at a chair without saying much. The kitchen smelled of warm butter and salty cheese, and a small radio in the corner was playing slow Georgian music. While she was rolling out the dough on a wooden table, she told me, in slow English, that she was making khachapuri for me because 'first night, must be cheese bread.' I tried to help, but she gently pushed me back into my chair. I watched her shape the dough into a boat, fill it with white cheese, and slide it into the oven. Twenty minutes later, she pulled out a golden, bubbling bread with a soft yellow yolk on top. She tore a corner with her fingers and handed it to me. I burned my tongue and laughed, and she laughed too. We did not share many words that night, but we shared a plate. Years later, when I cook khachapuri in my own kitchen, I always burn the first bite on purpose.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for scene-setting
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'was already standing at the stove' và 'was playing slow Georgian music' dùng past continuous để tạo khung cảnh tại thời điểm tôi bước vào.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Was already standing at the stove' and 'was playing slow Georgian music' use past continuous to frame the scene at the moment I walked in.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple chain for sequence
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi mô tả các bước (shape, fill, slide), dùng past simple cho từng bước cụ thể; past continuous chỉ dùng cho việc đang xảy ra trong nền.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When describing steps (shape, fill, slide), past simple marks each specific step; past continuous is reserved for the background of those steps.

sampleTranslationVi: Vào buổi tối đầu tiên ở Tbilisi, bà chủ nhà Nino đã đang đứng ở bếp khi tôi bước vào với chiếc va li. Bà chào tôi bằng một bàn tay đặt trên mỗi má và chỉ vào một chiếc ghế mà không nói nhiều. Căn bếp thơm mùi bơ ấm và phô mai mặn, và một chiếc đài nhỏ ở góc đang phát nhạc Gruzia chậm rãi. Trong khi bà đang cán bột trên bàn gỗ, bà nói với tôi bằng tiếng Anh chậm rằng bà đang làm khachapuri cho tôi vì 'đêm đầu tiên, phải là bánh phô mai.' Tôi cố giúp, nhưng bà nhẹ nhàng đẩy tôi ngồi lại ghế. Tôi nhìn bà tạo hình bột thành hình con thuyền, đổ phô mai trắng vào, và đưa nó vào lò. Hai mươi phút sau, bà lấy ra một ổ bánh vàng ươm, sôi tăm với lòng đỏ trứng mềm bên trên. Bà xé một góc bằng tay và đưa cho tôi. Tôi làm bỏng lưỡi và cười, và bà cũng cười. Đêm đó chúng tôi không nói nhiều, nhưng chia nhau một đĩa. Nhiều năm sau, khi tôi nấu khachapuri trong bếp của chính mình, tôi luôn cố ý làm cháy miếng đầu tiên.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past continuous opening sentence.
options: My host mother already standing at the stove when I came in.
My host mother was already stood at the stove when I came in.
My host mother was already standing at the stove when I came in.
My host mother is already standing at the stove when I came in.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was standing' for an ongoing situation at the moment 'I came in' (past simple).

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural past simple action sentence.
options: She greet me with a hand on each cheek.
She is greeted me with a hand on each cheek.
She has greet me with a hand on each cheek.
She greeted me with a hand on each cheek.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'greeted' for the finished welcome.

options: a soft mixture of flour and water used to make bread
the inside of a loaf of bread
a kind of cheese
a cooking pot

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While she rolled out the dough, she was told me about her village.
While she was rolling out the dough, she told me about her village.
While she rolls out the dough, she told me about her village.
While she is rolling out the dough, she told me about her village.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous for background; past simple for the inserted event 'told'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about smell.
options: The kitchen smell of warm butter and salty cheese.
The kitchen was smell of warm butter and salty cheese.
The kitchen smelled of warm butter and salty cheese.
The kitchen smelled to warm butter and salty cheese.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Smell of + noun' is the natural collocation; past simple 'smelled'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural past simple sentence with 'slide into'.
options: She slided it into the oven.
She slid it on the oven.
She was slide it into the oven.
She slid it into the oven.
mcQuestions.explanation: Irregular past simple: slide → slid; 'into' for movement to inside.

options: to pull something into pieces with your hands
to cry
to cook in oil
to push gently

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple irregular: 'I _____ my tongue and laughed.'
options: burned
Both A and B are acceptable
burnt
burning
mcQuestions.explanation: Both 'burned' and 'burnt' are accepted past simple forms in English.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural sentence about hand-feeding.
options: She teared a corner with her fingers and handed it to me.
She tore a corner by her fingers and handed it to me.
She tore a corner with her fingers and handed it to me.
She tear a corner with her fingers and handed to me.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple of 'tear' is 'tore'. 'Hand something to someone' is the natural phrase.
mcQuestions.hint: Past simple of 'tear'; hand + to + person.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'on purpose'.
options: I always burn the first bite on the purpose.
I always burn the first bite for purpose.
I always burn the first bite by purpose.
I always burn the first bite on purpose.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'On purpose' = intentionally. Fixed phrase, no article.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural closing sentence.
options: We did not share many words that night, but we shared a plate.
We did not shared many words that night, but we shared a plate.
We do not share many words that night, but we share a plate.
We did not share much words that night, but we shared a plate.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple negative 'did not share' (base form). 'Words' is countable, so 'many', not 'much'.
mcQuestions.hint: Did + not + base verb; many + plural.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why does food cooked for you, rather than served from a menu, change the way you remember a place?
